qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Service worker handleClick uses push-notification URLs without scope validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing scope validation in the Angular service worker push-notification handler (packages/service-worker/worker/src/driver.ts), enabling open redirect, CSRF, and navigation hijacking. The advisory describes an XSS vulnerability caused by i18n attribute bindings bypassing Angular's built-in sanitization in @angular/core and @angular/compiler. The root causes, vulnerable code paths, components, and vulnerability classes (open redirect/SSRF vs. XSS via sanitization bypass) are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes an XSS vulnerability where i18n attribute bindings (i18n-<attribute>) on security-sensitive attributes bypass Angular's built-in sanitization in @angular/core and @angular/compiler. The sole qualifying finding identifies a different vulnerability in a different component: missing scope validation in the Angular service worker push-notification handler (packages/service-worker/worker/src/driver.ts), which is an open redirect/SSRF issue. The root causes, vulnerable code, affected components, and vulnerability classes are all distinct.
evidence: Advisory root cause: i18n-<attribute> directive bypasses sanitization for security-sensitive attributes like href, src, etc.
Finding root cause: handleClick in service worker driver.ts constructs URLs from push notification data without scope validation
No overlap in vulnerable function, class, file, endpoint, sanitization bypass mechanism, or control-flow path
